label: Hamas Rocket Barrage — Be'er Sheva
notes: Oct 7 opening barrage; ~3,000 rockets fired in first 24 hours toward multiple Israeli cities

label: Hamas Rockets — Ashkelon
notes: Oct 7 attack; Ashkelon is 15km from northern Gaza

label: Hamas Rockets — Tel Aviv Area
damage: Multiple impacts in greater Tel Aviv
notes: Oct 7; Hamas fired longest-range Ayyash rockets; Iron Dome largely effective

label: IDF Air Campaign — Gaza City (Oct 2023)
damage: Massive structural damage; northern Gaza largely destroyed by Nov 2023
notes: Israeli F-35I jets and F-16s conducted thousands of strikes; used 2,000-lb bombs in dense urban areas

label: IDF Ground Advance — Northern Gaza (Oct 2023)
notes: IDF 162nd Division and 36th Division entered from the north; cut off Gaza City by late October

label: IDF Ground Operation — Khan Yunis (Dec 2023)
damage: Large swaths of Khan Yunis destroyed
notes: Operation targeting Hamas's Khan Yunis brigade; IDF hunted Sinwar; operation lasted through April 2024

label: IDF Rafah Operation (May 2024)
damage: Rafah severely damaged; Philadelphi Corridor seized
notes: Despite US warnings, Israel advanced into Rafah. Rafah border crossing closed; ~1.4M displaced persons forced to move again

label: Aid Corridor — Kerem Shalom
notes: Main humanitarian aid route into Gaza; capacity severely limited. UN demanded 500+ trucks/day; actual throughput peaked ~200/day. Repeatedly closed by Israeli decisions and Hamas mortar attacks.

label: Hamas Breach — Be'eri Infiltration
notes: Hamas fighters breached fence and drove to kibbutzim; Be'eri was the bloodiest single community

label: Hamas Attack — Nova Music Festival
notes: Approximately 3,500 attendees at the trance music festival; Hamas gunmen attacked from multiple directions

label: IDF Operation — Jenin (2023-2025)
damage: Jenin camp partially destroyed; electrical/water infrastructure damaged
notes: IDF Operation Home and Garden (Jul 2023) — largest West Bank operation since 2002. Over 100 operations in Jenin/Tulkarm area through 2025.

label: PIJ / Hamas Rockets — Sderot
damage: Multiple structures hit; police station overrun on Oct 7
notes: Sderot is 1.5km from the Gaza fence; among the earliest-impacted Israeli communities

label: IDF Airstrikes — Rafah (May 2024)
notes: 26 May 2024 strike on humanitarian zone killed 45; US temporarily paused transfer of 2,000-lb bombs

label: Hamas Anti-Tank Fire — Northern Gaza
notes: Hamas employed RPGs, anti-tank guided missiles (Kornet), and IEDs against IDF Merkava tanks during urban operations

label: Israeli Navy — Coastal Bombardment
notes: Israeli Sa'ar 5 and Sa'ar 6 corvettes conducted offshore bombardments of Hamas maritime infrastructure and coastal targets

label: Egypt Aid Route — North Sinai to Rafah
notes: UN and NGO aid convoys via Al-Arish → Rafah. Egyptian military controlled access; entry into Gaza severely restricted. Egypt refused to absorb Palestinian refugees.

label: Hamas Long-Range Rocket — Haifa (2024)
notes: Hamas demonstrated longer-range capability firing towards Haifa during Oct 7 anniversary; most intercepted by Iron Dome

label: Al-Ahli Hospital Explosion (Oct 17, 2023)
notes: Major disputed event. US, Israeli, and multiple Western intelligence assessments concluded a misfired Palestinian Islamic Jihad rocket caused the explosion. Hamas and Arab media initially blamed an Israeli airstrike.

label: IDF Drone/Ground — Sinwar Elimination (Oct 16, 2024)
notes: Sinwar discovered by IDF ground patrol in Rafah during routine operations; engaged and killed. IDF drone also fired on him during the encounter. Confirmed by DNA testing.

label: Phase 1 Ceasefire Aid Surge (Jan–Mar 2025)
notes: During Phase 1 ceasefire, 600+ aid trucks per day entered Gaza. Humanitarian agencies reported partial easing of catastrophic conditions. Resumed operations in March 2025 again restricted aid.

label: Settler Rampage — Jalud & Qaryut Villages
casualties: 3 hospitalized with head wounds; multiple vehicle and property attacks
damage: 10+ homes torched, 5+ vehicles burned, village council building burned, clinic arson attempted, fire truck attacked
notes: ~100 masked settlers descended on Jalud and Qaryut (south of Nablus); attacks spread to Deir Sharaf, Deir al-Hatab, and Burqa; IDF present on outskirts but made no arrests

label: IDF Shooting — Workers' Vehicle Pursuit, Hebron Hills
notes: IDF soldiers fired at vehicles transporting Palestinian workers near military checkpoint in southern Hebron Hills; footage showed vehicles moving away from checkpoint; victim had reentered vehicle before being shot

label: Settler Shooting — Mukhmas (Palestinian-American Killed)
notes: Second Palestinian-American killed by Israeli settlers in under a year; village of Mukhmas northeast of Jerusalem; incident drew heightened attention due to victim's US citizenship

label: IDF Drone Strike — Bani Suheila, East Khan Younis
notes: IDF drone struck a car near the Bani Suheila roundabout east of Khan Younis; ceasefire violation cited; wounded taken to Nasser Hospital

label: IDF Airstrike — Hamas Operatives, Shejaiya (Gaza City)
casualties: 2 killed (Fahmi and Saed Qaddoum, Hamas Deterrent Force operatives)
notes: IAF targeted two Hamas operatives in Shejaiya neighborhood; Hamas Deterrent Force confirmed targets as resistance fighters; strike followed attack on IDF troops by armed operatives

label: Houthi Ballistic Missile — Negev (First Attack Since Nov 2025)
casualties: No casualties reported from this specific missile
notes: First Houthi ballistic missile attack on Israel since November 2025 ceasefire freeze; sirens sounded across Beersheba/Negev; air defense activated; Houthis claimed targeting 'sensitive Israeli military sites in southern occupied Palestine'; struck open area in the Negev

label: Iranian Missile Barrage (6 Salvos) — Central Israel / Beersheba (March 29)
casualties: No confirmed casualties; medics responded to industrial zone impact near Beersheba
notes: Six separate missile salvos fired by Iran targeting Israel as US-Israel war on Iran enters second month; sirens across central Israel and West Bank; Arrow 2/3 and David's Sling batteries activated; Strait of Hormuz remains closed

label: Houthi Second Wave — Missiles/Drones (Within 24 Hours of First Attack)
notes: Second Houthi attack on Israel within 24 hours of resuming operations on March 28; mix of ballistic missiles and drones; all intercepted by Israeli air defense; Houthi spokesman pledged continued operations in solidarity with Iran, Gaza, Lebanon

label: Hezbollah Rocket Fire — Tiberias / Galilee (Coordinated Multi-Front Attack)
notes: Hezbollah fired rockets at Tiberias and northern Israeli towns coinciding with Iranian missile salvos — part of coordinated Axis of Resistance multi-front assault designed to overwhelm Israeli air defense; sirens sounded across Galilee

label: IDF Airstrikes — Police Checkpoints, Southern Khan Younis (March 29)
notes: Israeli airstrikes on two police checkpoints in southern Khan Younis; IDF claimed targets were armed Hamas-affiliated operatives; Gaza MoH confirmed fatalities and transfers to Nasser Hospital

label: IDF Airstrike — Two Brothers Killed Near Showa Roundabout, Shejaiya (March 29)
notes: Two Palestinian brothers killed in IDF strike near the Showa roundabout in Shejaiya, Gaza City; second IDF strike in Shejaiya area in two days; area has been target of intermittent IDF operations despite ceasefire

label: IDF Airstrike — Zeitoun Neighborhood, Gaza City (March 30)
notes: IDF targeted alleged Hamas armed cell in Zeitoun neighborhood; dead and wounded transferred to Al-Shifa and Al-Ahli hospitals; part of continuing low-level operations despite October 2025 ceasefire

label: Iranian Missile Barrage — Negev / Central Israel (Day 31, March 30)
notes: Day 31 Iranian ballistic missile barrage; Arrow 2/3 and David's Sling activated; at least one impact wounded 10 Israelis; IDF conducted ~230 strikes on Iranian facilities in preceding 24 hours; HRW confirmed Iran used cluster munitions in prior strikes on Yehud and Ramat Gan

label: IDF Airstrikes — Gaza City (Two Attacks, March 31)
notes: Two separate Israeli strikes in Gaza on March 31 killed at least 5 Palestinians; dead transferred to Al-Nasser and Al-Ahli hospitals; IDF cited ongoing Hamas ceasefire violations

label: Iranian Ballistic Missile — Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Zone, Negev (March 29)
damage: Strike on chemical industrial zone; hazmat emergency protocols activated; evacuation ordered
notes: Iranian ballistic missile bypassed Israeli air defense and struck Ne'ot Hovav (Ramat Hovav) chemical industrial complex 12km south of Beersheba; hazmat teams deployed over fears of toxic chemical release; second Iranian air defense penetration in two days after Eshtaol strike
